title: Bias-variance, double descent, learning curves
focus: The framing every model debugging conversation falls back on.

items.label: Bias-variance decomposition with worked example
interviewQuestions: Decompose expected squared error into bias², variance, and irreducible noise.
Why does adding more training data reduce variance but not bias?

items.label: Double descent: when more parameters help
interviewQuestions: Explain the double-descent phenomenon. How does it overturn classical bias-variance intuition?
Why do over-parameterized models often generalize well in deep learning?

items.label: Learning curves: diagnose underfit vs overfit
interviewQuestions: How do you read a learning curve to decide between more data, regularization, or a bigger model?
What does a large gap between training and validation curves usually mean — and what shrinks it?
Decompose expected squared error into bias², variance, and irreducible noise — give an example of each.
How would you diagnose whether to add more data, regularize harder, or grow the model from a learning curve?
Explain double descent — why does it overturn the classical bias-variance picture?
